root_text.an8.10:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā campāyaṁ viharati gaggarāya pokkharaṇiyā tīre.
root_text.an8.10:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhū bhikkhuṁ āpattiyā codenti.
root_text.an8.10:1.3: So bhikkhu bhikkhūhi āpattiyā codiyamāno aññenāññaṁ paṭicarati, bahiddhā kathaṁ apanāmeti, kopañca dosañca appaccayañca pātukaroti.
root_text.an8.10:2.6: Idha, bhikkhave, ekaccassa puggalassa tādisaṁyeva hoti abhikkantaṁ paṭikkantaṁ ālokitaṁ vilokitaṁ samiñjitaṁ pasāritaṁ saṅghāṭipattacīvaradhāraṇaṁ, seyyathāpi aññesaṁ bhaddakānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ—
root_text.an8.10:2.8: Yato ca khvassa bhikkhū āpattiṁ passanti, tamenaṁ evaṁ jānanti:
root_text.an8.10:3.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, sampanne yavakaraṇe yavadūsī jāyetha yavapalāpo yavakāraṇḍavoti.
root_text.an8.10:3.2: Tassa tādisaṁyeva mūlaṁ hoti, seyyathāpi aññesaṁ bhaddakānaṁ yavānaṁ;
root_text.an8.10:3.3: tādisaṁyeva nāḷaṁ hoti, seyyathāpi aññesaṁ bhaddakānaṁ yavānaṁ;
root_text.an8.10:3.4: tādisaṁyeva pattaṁ hoti, seyyathāpi aññesaṁ bhaddakānaṁ yavānaṁ—
root_text.an8.10:3.6: Yato ca khvassa sīsaṁ nibbattati, tamenaṁ evaṁ jānanti:
root_text.an8.10:3.8: Tamenaṁ iti viditvā samūlaṁ uppāṭetvā bahiddhā yavakaraṇassa chaḍḍenti.
root_text.an8.10:4.1: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, idhekaccassa puggalassa tādisaṁyeva hoti abhikkantaṁ paṭikkantaṁ ālokitaṁ vilokitaṁ samiñjitaṁ pasāritaṁ saṅghāṭipattacīvaradhāraṇaṁ, seyyathāpi aññesaṁ bhaddakānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ—
root_text.an8.10:4.3: Yato ca khvassa bhikkhū āpattiṁ passanti, tamenaṁ evaṁ jānanti:
root_text.an8.10:5.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, mahato dhaññarāsissa phuṇamānassa tattha yāni tāni dhaññāni daḷhāni sāravantāni tāni ekamantaṁ puñjaṁ hoti, yāni pana tāni dhaññāni dubbalāni palāpāni tāni vāto ekamantaṁ apavahati.
root_text.an8.10:5.2: Tamenaṁ sāmikā sammajjaniṁ gahetvā bhiyyoso mattāya apasammajjanti.
root_text.an8.10:5.5: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, idhekaccassa puggalassa tādisaṁyeva hoti abhikkantaṁ paṭikkantaṁ ālokitaṁ vilokitaṁ samiñjitaṁ pasāritaṁ saṅghāṭipattacīvaradhāraṇaṁ, seyyathāpi aññesaṁ bhaddakānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ—
root_text.an8.10:5.7: Yato ca khvassa bhikkhū āpattiṁ passanti, tamenaṁ evaṁ jānanti:
root_text.an8.10:6.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, puriso udapānapanāḷiyatthiko tiṇhaṁ kuṭhāriṁ ādāya vanaṁ paviseyya.
root_text.an8.10:6.2: So yaṁ yadeva rukkhaṁ kuṭhāripāsena ākoṭeyya tattha yāni tāni rukkhāni daḷhāni sāravantāni tāni kuṭhāripāsena ākoṭitāni kakkhaḷaṁ paṭinadanti;
root_text.an8.10:6.3: yāni pana tāni rukkhāni antopūtīni avassutāni kasambujātāni tāni kuṭhāripāsena ākoṭitāni daddaraṁ paṭinadanti.
root_text.an8.10:6.4: Tamenaṁ mūle chindati, mūle chinditvā agge chindati, agge chinditvā anto suvisodhitaṁ visodheti, anto suvisodhitaṁ visodhetvā udapānapanāḷiṁ yojeti.
root_text.an8.10:6.5: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, idhekaccassa puggalassa tādisaṁyeva hoti abhikkantaṁ paṭikkantaṁ ālokitaṁ vilokitaṁ samiñjitaṁ pasāritaṁ saṅghāṭipattacīvaradhāraṇaṁ, seyyathāpi aññesaṁ bhaddakānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ—
root_text.an8.10:6.7: Yato ca khvassa bhikkhū āpattiṁ passanti, tamenaṁ evaṁ jānanti:

translation_text.an8.10:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Campā on the banks of the Gaggarā Lotus Pond.
translation_text.an8.10:1.2: Now at that time the mendicants accused a mendicant of an offense.
translation_text.an8.10:1.3: The accused mendicant dodged the issue, distracted the discussion with irrelevant points, and displayed annoyance, hate, and bitterness.
translation_text.an8.10:2.1: Then the Buddha said to the mendicants,
translation_text.an8.10:2.4: This individual should be shown the door.
translation_text.an8.10:2.5: Why should you be vexed by an outsider?
translation_text.an8.10:2.6: Take a case where a certain individual looks just the same as other good-natured mendicants when going out and coming back, when looking ahead and aside, when bending and extending the limbs, and when bearing the outer robe, bowl and robes.
translation_text.an8.10:2.7: That is, so long as the mendicants don’t notice his offense.
translation_text.an8.10:2.8: But when the mendicants notice the offense, they know that he’s
translation_text.an8.10:2.9: a corrupt ascetic, just useless trash.
translation_text.an8.10:2.10: When they realize this they send him away.
translation_text.an8.10:2.12: So that he doesn’t corrupt good-natured mendicants.
translation_text.an8.10:3.1: Suppose in a growing field of barley some bad barley appeared, just useless trash.
translation_text.an8.10:3.2: Its roots, stem, and leaves would look just the same as the healthy barley.
translation_text.an8.10:3.5: That is, so long as the head doesn’t appear.
translation_text.an8.10:3.6: But when the head appears, they know that it’s
translation_text.an8.10:3.8: When they realize this they pull it up by the roots and throw it outside the field.
translation_text.an8.10:3.10: So that it doesn’t spoil the good barley.
translation_text.an8.10:4.1: In the same way, take a case where a certain individual looks just the same as other good-natured mendicants when going out and coming back, when looking ahead and aside, when bending and extending the limbs, and when bearing the outer robe, bowl and robes.
translation_text.an8.10:4.2: That is, so long as the mendicants don’t notice his offense.
translation_text.an8.10:4.3: But when the mendicants notice the offense, they know that he’s
translation_text.an8.10:4.4: a corrupt ascetic, just useless trash.
translation_text.an8.10:4.5: When they realize this they send him away.
translation_text.an8.10:4.7: So that he doesn’t corrupt good-natured mendicants.
translation_text.an8.10:5.1: Suppose that a large heap of grain is being winnowed. The grains that are firm and solid form a heap on one side. And the grains that are flimsy and insubstantial are blown over to the other side.
translation_text.an8.10:5.2: The owners take a broom and sweep them even further away.
translation_text.an8.10:5.4: So that it doesn’t spoil the good grain.
translation_text.an8.10:5.5: In the same way, take a case where a certain individual looks just the same as other good-natured mendicants when going out and coming back, when looking ahead and aside, when bending and extending the limbs, and when bearing the outer robe, bowl and robes.
translation_text.an8.10:5.6: That is, so long as the mendicants don’t notice his offense.
translation_text.an8.10:5.7: But when the mendicants notice the offense, they know that he’s
translation_text.an8.10:5.8: a corrupt ascetic, just useless trash.
translation_text.an8.10:5.9: When they realize this they send him away.
translation_text.an8.10:5.11: So that he doesn’t corrupt good-natured mendicants.
translation_text.an8.10:6.1: Suppose a man needs an irrigation gutter for a well. He’d take a sharp axe and enter the wood,
translation_text.an8.10:6.2: where he’d knock various trees with the axe. The trees that were firm and solid made a cracking sound.
translation_text.an8.10:6.3: But the trees that were rotten inside, decomposing and decayed, made a thud.
translation_text.an8.10:6.4: He’d cut down such a tree at the root, lop off the crown, and thoroughly clear out the insides. Then he’d use it as an irrigation gutter for the well.
translation_text.an8.10:6.5: In the same way, take a case where a certain individual looks just the same as other good-natured mendicants when going out and coming back, when looking ahead and aside, when bending and extending the limbs, and when bearing the outer robe, bowl and robes.
translation_text.an8.10:6.6: That is, so long as the mendicants don’t notice his offense.
translation_text.an8.10:6.7: But when the mendicants notice the offense, they know that he’s
translation_text.an8.10:6.8: a corrupt ascetic, just useless trash.
translation_text.an8.10:6.9: When they realize this they send him away.
translation_text.an8.10:6.11: So that he doesn’t corrupt good-natured mendicants.
translation_text.an8.10:8.1: They speak to people with a voice
translation_text.an8.10:8.2: so smooth, just like an ascetic.
translation_text.an8.10:8.3: But they act in secret, with their bad views
translation_text.an8.10:8.4: and their lack of regard for others.
translation_text.an8.10:9.1: You should recognize them for what they are:
translation_text.an8.10:10.4: they’re not ascetics, they just think they are.
translation_text.an8.10:11.1: When you’ve thrown out those of corrupt wishes,

variant_text.an8.10:2.5: tena paraputtena visodhitena → kiṁ vo paraputto viheṭhiyati (bj); kiṁ paraputto viheṭheti (sya-all); kiṁ vo paraputtā viheṭheti (pts1ed); kiṁ so paraputto visodheti (mr)
variant_text.an8.10:2.9: samaṇadūsīvāyaṁ → samaṇarūpī cāyaṁ (mr) | samaṇakāraṇḍavo’ti → samaṇakaraṇḍavoti (sya-all, mr)
variant_text.an8.10:5.1: phuṇamānassa → pūyamānassa (bj); vuyhamānassa (si); phusayamānassa (sya-all); vuvahyamānassa (pts1ed); punamānassa (?) | apavahati → apakassati (bj)
